title: ipomoea crassipes var. hirta
intro: Ipomoea crassipes var. hirta, commonly known as the American water-hemp or hairy Ipomoea, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the morning glory family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Ipomoea crassipes var. hirta include reducing inflammation, relieving fever, and treating various infections. Traditional herbal preparations involve using the plant's roots or leaves to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat various ailments.
